description:
Advances: specled.evidence_store, specled.mix_tasks

Deliverable:
1. SpecLedEx.Evidence.Sync (lib/specled_ex/evidence/sync.ex): fetch remote ref (`git fetch origin +refs/heads/spec-evidence:refs/remotes/origin/spec-evidence`) -> per-key tree-union merge via plumbing ONLY (union of entry paths from local and remote evidence trees; same-path/different-blob resolved by reading both entries' stamps and keeping max — identical rule to S2 local replace) -> assemble merged tree under a unique GIT_INDEX_FILE -> commit-tree (parents: local tip + remote tip when both exist; unrelated self-created roots merge identically — reconciliation is content-based, never history-based) -> update local ref -> push `--force-with-lease=refs/heads/spec-evidence:<fetched-sha>`; on lease rejection refetch -> re-merge -> retry: 3 attempts, 250ms x attempt jittered. Remote-absent -> plain push; local-absent -> adopt remote. Orphan ref never checked out.
2. mix spec.sync (lib/mix/tasks/spec.sync.ex): default mode raises (Mix.raise, non-zero) on persistent failure; --best-effort flag emits exactly one well-formed warning and exits 0 (the hook's mode). Output surfaces ahead/behind entry counts labeled "as of last fetch" (drift surfacing Should-Have).
3. mix spec.prune (lib/mix/tasks/spec.prune.ex): fetch, compute live tree-hash set (`git log --format=%T` over local branch heads + remote-tracking refs), drop unreachable entries, commit + CAS-push via the same Sync path. Explicit-invocation only; moduledoc warns against pruning during active review windows.
4. Spec amendments: evidence_store.spec.md — flip the sync-group verification stub (sync_tree_union, sync_failure_contracts, prune_explicit_only, drift_surfaced) execute:false->true as tests land; mix_tasks.spec.md gains the two new tasks.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: new test/specled_ex/evidence/sync_test.exs, test/mix/tasks/spec_sync_task_test.exs, test/mix/tasks/spec_prune_task_test.exs. Flip evidence_store sync-group stub.
- Behaviors to demonstrate (scenario IDs from evidence_store.spec.md): scenario.sync_converges_in_both_push_orders (two clones of one bare origin; disjoint-entry union; same-key conflict resolved by constructed stamps in BOTH push orders — order-independence asserted; unrelated self-created roots converge; lease rejection -> refetch-retry succeeds; no worktree ever checks out spec-evidence); scenario.sync_exhaustion_modes (default raises, --best-effort exactly one warning + exit 0); scenario.prune_drops_only_unreachable (reachable via local head kept, via remote-tracking kept, unreachable dropped; plain sync never pruned).
- Verification cadence:
  - Iteration tier: compile-graph-scoped checks only (mix test --stale or named files) plus mix spec.check --no-run-commands.
  - Final tier: full merge-gate suite run-until-green before IMPLEMENTED.
  - Verifier tier: flipped stubs + targeted/--stale only; no full suite.
- Standing bounce-class checks:
  - Production call site named for each new callable surface, with proving test on that production path
  - Fixture hygiene: bare-origin + two-clone fixture repos torn down; no cross-test leakage
  - Spec surfaces fresh for every touched callable/config surface
  - Every conditional clause covered (remote-absent, local-absent, lease-reject, exhaustion, both merge parent shapes)
- Call-site Integration: Evidence.Sync.run/2 called from Mix.Tasks.Spec.Sync.run/1 (and later the S5 hook shim); proven by spec_sync_task_test.exs driving the task against a bare-origin + two-clone fixture through the real push/lease path.

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/evidence/{tree_hash,entry,store}.ex (S2, done — read-only deps)
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex (S2 done; S5 changes it)
- lib/specled_ex/review/** (S4)
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.evidence.*, priv/hooks/** (S5)
- docs/**, priv/spec_init/**, skills/**, .gitignore
Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- lib/specled_ex/evidence/sync.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.sync.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.prune.ex
- test/specled_ex/evidence/sync_test.exs
- test/mix/tasks/spec_sync_task_test.exs
- test/mix/tasks/spec_prune_task_test.exs
- .spec/specs/evidence_store.spec.md (stub flips execute:false->true; amendments only via ADR)
- .spec/specs/mix_tasks.spec.md (two new tasks + stub flips)
- .spec/state.json, .spec/realization_hashes.json (tool-managed regen — NOT scope creep)

Out of Scope (intent):
- spec.check stays zero-network — do NOT auto-invoke sync from check (pinned).
- No hook installation (S5); spec.sync is hand-invocable from this stage on.
- No prune-on-sync — sync must never delete entries.
- No history-based merge logic anywhere; content/tree-union only.
- Escalation valve: if --force-with-lease semantics against non-tracking refs surface a genuine unknown on the CI git version, ESCALATE (mega-plan trigger), do not work around in place.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly (mix compile --warnings-as-errors)
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] Spec verification passes (mix spec.check; adjudicate against S1 baseline note)
- [ ] mix format --check-formatted; mix deps.unlock --check-unused
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: codex-551b2c74
Log: /Users/mz/.local/state/agentic-runs/specled_ex/specled_-csg.3/codex-551b2c74/log

Commit: 4e60e97ef22124c3f6279a0e7b046b3933cf0f60
Files touched: .spec/realization_hashes.json, .spec/specs/evidence_store.spec.md, .spec/specs/mix_tasks.spec.md, .spec/state.json, lib/mix/tasks/spec.prune.ex, lib/mix/tasks/spec.sync.ex, lib/specled_ex/evidence/sync.ex, test/mix/tasks/spec_prune_task_test.exs, test/mix/tasks/spec_sync_task_test.exs, test/specled_ex/evidence/sync_test.exs
Tests added: SpecLedEx.Evidence.SyncTest — remote-absent pushes and local-absent adopts without checking out the evidence ref; tree union converges in both push orders and higher same-key stamp wins; lease rejection refetches, re-merges, and succeeds; three consecutive lease rejections exhaust the bounded retry. Mix.Tasks.SpecSyncTaskTest — task drives the production Sync.run/2 path through a real bare-origin push; default raises while best-effort emits exactly one warning and returns ok. Mix.Tasks.SpecPruneTaskTest — explicit prune keeps local-head and remote-tracking trees and drops only unreachable.
Verification stubs flipped: specled.evidence_store sync-group command stub covering specled.evidence_store.sync_tree_union, specled.evidence_store.sync_failure_contracts, specled.evidence_store.prune_explicit_only, specled.evidence_store.drift_surfaced

Summary: Added plumbing-only, content-based evidence synchronization with deterministic run-stamp conflict resolution, local/remote absence handling, bounded jittered force-with-lease retries, and drift counts labeled as of the last fetch. Added production mix spec.sync and explicit-only mix spec.prune paths with default/best-effort failure contracts, reachability-based pruning, fresh subject specs, and hermetic bare-origin/two-clone tests; the full 987-test suite and command-executing spec check pass.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: 8384310d-16ba-4102-b635-60dea30661d7
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/8384310d-16ba-4102-b635-60dea30661d7.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 10
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0
  (All 10 map to Allowed: sync.ex, spec.sync.ex, spec.prune.ex, the three new
  test files, evidence_store.spec.md, mix_tasks.spec.md, and the tool-managed
  .spec/state.json + .spec/realization_hashes.json regen — the latter two are
  pure additive regen reflecting the two new requirements and two new callables,
  no hand-edited requirement bodies.)

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 2 flipped command stubs (union = 3 test files), passed: all.
    - evidence_store sync-group: mix test sync_test.exs spec_sync_task_test.exs spec_prune_task_test.exs
    - mix_tasks sync/prune: mix test spec_sync_task_test.exs spec_prune_task_test.exs
  Combined run: 7 tests, 0 failures.

- Requirements coverage (one line per must/should this ticket advances):
  - specled.evidence_store.sync_tree_union (must): covered by sync_test.exs:30
    ("both push orders"), :13 (remote/local absence), :57 (lease refetch-remerge).
    Would fail if merge were history-based or order-dependent — b-first order
    would leave hash("3").run_id == "a"; assertion sync_test.exs:44-45 catches it.
    Two-parent commit shape asserted at :46-51 (%P split == 2).
  - specled.evidence_store.sync_failure_contracts (must): covered by sync_test.exs:80
    (3 rejections -> {:error,{:sync_exhausted}}) + spec_sync_task_test.exs:39.
    Would fail if the 3-attempt bound were off — sync_test.exs:99 would not match
    {:error,{:sync_exhausted,_}} (unbounded retry would hang / eventually succeed).
  - specled.evidence_store.prune_explicit_only (must): covered by spec_prune_task_test.exs:17.
    Would fail if plain sync pruned — assertion :36 (plain Sync.run keeps all three
    incl. unreachable) catches prune-on-sync; :39 catches dropping reachable /
    keeping unreachable.
  - specled.evidence_store.drift_surfaced (should): covered by spec_sync_task_test.exs:19.
    Would fail if ahead/behind not surfaced+labeled — message assertion :34
    ("drift as of last fetch: ahead=1 behind=0") catches it.
  - specled.mix_tasks / specled.tasks.sync_evidence (must): covered by
    spec_sync_task_test.exs both tests. Would fail if task bypassed production
    Sync.run — real ls-remote :35 proves the ref was pushed; :44/:53 prove
    default-raise vs best-effort-one-warning-exit-0.
  - specled.mix_tasks / specled.tasks.prune_evidence (must): covered by
    spec_prune_task_test.exs:17. Would fail if prune skipped the pre-fetch — the
    remote-only tree (reachable only via origin/remote-only fetched at :26) would
    be dropped, failing :39.

- Intent-level scope concerns: none violated.
    - spec.check stays zero-network: spec.check.ex NOT in diff; sync is hand-invoked only.
    - No hook install: priv/hooks/** and install_hook untouched.
    - No prune-on-sync: keep_entries(entries, nil) -> entries; :keep MapSet passed
      only by the prune task, never by plain Sync.run. Proven by prune test :35-36.
    - Content/tree-union only: reconcile = Map.merge + Entry.latest on entry paths;
      commit parents set lineage but never drive the merge decision. No history walk.
    - Escalation valve: force-with-lease uses the explicit
      --force-with-lease=refs/heads/spec-evidence:<fetched-sha> form, which is the
      standard git mechanism for non-tracking refs (remote-tracking ref is fetched
      manually into refs/remotes/origin/spec-evidence). No genuine unknown was
      worked around in place; nothing to escalate.
    - Orphan ref never checked out: grep for checkout/switch/worktree in the three
      new source files = none; all git ops are plumbing (ls-tree/cat-file/hash-object/
      write-tree/commit-tree/update-ref/push). Tests also assert current_branch != spec-evidence.

- Targeted verification:
    - Flipped stubs run (above): 7 tests, 0 failures.
    - mix format --check-formatted on all 6 touched code/test files: clean.
    - dep_runtime_bootstrap: both new tasks contain SpecLedEx.MixRuntime.ensure_started (grep -L empty).
    - make smoke: green (compile --warnings-as-errors clean, no recompile).
- Full-suite verification: not run by verifier (by design).
- Audit-expanded scope (step 11): none (no prior AUDIT comments on this ticket).

- Call-site trace: Evidence.Sync.run/2 <- Mix.Tasks.Spec.Sync.run/1 (spec.sync.ex:30),
  and prune calls Sync.fetch + Sync.run(root, keep:) (spec.prune.ex:27-29). The task
  tests exercise the REAL bare-origin push/lease path (remote_repo!/fixture! set up a
  bare origin + real push), not a seam mock — the before_push seam appears only in the
  unit sync_test.exs to inject a competing writer; the production push/fetch/lease all
  execute unmocked. Both push orders asserted with constructed string stamps (no
  wall-clock). Merge-parent shapes: two-parent (sync_test.exs:46), single-parent
  remote-absent (absence test), local-absent adopt, and prune same-commit all exercised.

Verdict: PASS

Notes (advisory, do not change verdict):
- The commit_tree(root, tree, :absent, remote) clause (prune when the local ref is
  absent but remote exists) is a defensive edge not directly exercised by any test.
  It is outside the required scenario set and does not affect any covered requirement's
  literal contract, so it is a Note, not a Gap.
- push_race?/1 matches on git stderr substrings ("stale info"/"fetch first"/
  "non-fast-forward"/"rejected"). This is git-version-text-dependent but is the
  conventional detection approach and the lease-reject path is proven end-to-end
  against the real git in this environment; advisory only.

AUDIT: APPROVE (auto — selective audit)
Session: 8384310d-16ba-4102-b635-60dea30661d7
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/8384310d-16ba-4102-b635-60dea30661d7.jsonl

Reasoning: VERIFY reported PASS with full stub coverage, full requirement coverage,
zero mechanical or intent-level scope violations, and passing project verification.
No auditor was spawned per selective-audit policy.
APPROVED. Merged into specled_-csg/evidence-ledger-split (ff to 4e60e97). Closing per orchestrator sign-off.

title: S3: Sync machinery: spec.sync + spec.prune + drift surfacing
